title_en: Worship Hadiths and Their Legal Significance
content_en:
The worship-related hadiths in the Sahih of Ibn Hibban cover the same broad range of devotional obligations as the canonical collections — purification, prayer, almsgiving, fasting, and pilgrimage — but they often preserve the material through different chains or in different wordings that supplement what is found in al-Bukhari and Muslim. This supplementary quality is precisely what makes Ibn Hibban's collection valuable in advanced hadith scholarship, since it allows researchers to assess the strength of a tradition across multiple chains of transmission.

The purification sections of the Sahih preserve many detailed rulings through chains with Khorasani authority that differ from the primarily Medinan and Iraqi chains that dominate the two primary Sahihs. This regional diversity of transmission is important for establishing the widespread observance of particular practices across the early Muslim community, strengthening the case that certain practices were universal rather than local. Ibn Hibban's inclusion of these chains makes his Sahih an important complement to the canonical two even on topics that appear to be thoroughly covered elsewhere.

The prayer sections are similarly rich. Ibn Hibban preserved authenticated traditions on the voluntary prayers, the night prayer, and the special prayers of particular occasions that add depth and detail to the picture of prophetic prayer practice. His coverage of the congregational prayer, the Friday prayer, and the prayer in congregation is extensive, and several important rulings find their clearest prophetic basis in traditions preserved in his Sahih that are not found in quite the same form in the primary collections.

The fasting and pilgrimage sections of the Sahih are also notable for the additional coverage they provide beyond what appears in al-Bukhari and Muslim. The pilgrimage sections in particular preserve detailed narrations about the rituals of hajj and umrah through chains that complement those in the canonical sources. For scholars engaged in the study of comparative fiqh, the ability to cite from the Sahih of Ibn Hibban alongside the primary Sahihs provides an important additional layer of evidentiary support.
content_id:
Hadis-hadis yang berkaitan dengan ibadah dalam Sahih Ibn Hibban mencakup rentang kewajiban ibadah yang sama luasnya dengan koleksi-koleksi kanonik: taharah, salat, zakat, puasa, dan haji, namun sering melestarikan materi melalui sanad-sanad yang berbeda atau dalam redaksi-redaksi yang berbeda yang melengkapi apa yang terdapat dalam Al-Bukhari dan Muslim. Kualitas pelengkap inilah yang menjadikan koleksi Ibn Hibban sangat berharga dalam keilmuan hadis tingkat lanjut, karena memungkinkan para peneliti menilai kekuatan sebuah riwayat di berbagai sanad transmisi.

Bagian-bagian taharah dalam Sahih melestarikan banyak hukum-hukum terperinci melalui sanad-sanad dengan otoritas Khorasani yang berbeda dari sanad-sanad yang terutama Madinawi dan Irak yang mendominasi dua Sahih primer. Keragaman regional transmisi ini penting untuk menetapkan bahwa praktik-praktik tertentu dijalankan secara luas di seluruh komunitas Muslim awal, memperkuat argumen bahwa praktik-praktik tertentu bersifat universal bukan lokal. Pencantuman sanad-sanad ini oleh Ibn Hibban menjadikan Sahih-nya pelengkap penting bagi dua Sahih kanonik bahkan pada topik-topik yang tampaknya sudah dibahas secara menyeluruh di tempat lain.

Bagian-bagian salat juga sama kayanya. Ibn Hibban melestarikan riwayat-riwayat sahih tentang salat-salat sunnah, salat malam (tahajud), dan salat-salat khusus pada kesempatan-kesempatan tertentu yang menambah kedalaman dan detail pada gambaran praktik salat kenabian. Cakupannya atas salat berjamaah, salat Jumat, dan salat dalam jemaah sangat luas, dan beberapa hukum penting menemukan landasan kenabian yang paling jelas dalam riwayat-riwayat yang dilestarikan dalam Sahih-nya yang tidak ditemukan dalam bentuk yang persis sama dalam koleksi-koleksi primer.

Bagian-bagian puasa dan haji dalam Sahih juga patut dicatat untuk cakupan tambahan yang mereka berikan di luar apa yang muncul dalam Al-Bukhari dan Muslim. Bagian-bagian haji khususnya melestarikan riwayat-riwayat terperinci tentang manasik haji dan umrah melalui sanad-sanad yang melengkapi sanad-sanad dalam sumber-sumber kanonik. Bagi ulama yang terlibat dalam kajian fikih komparatif, kemampuan untuk mengutip dari Sahih Ibn Hibban bersama Sahih-Sahih primer memberikan lapisan dukungan pembuktian tambahan yang penting.
